mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, prompting market pricing of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which reduces S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during CPIAUCSL acceleration periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, causing equity valuations to contract over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median 20-day returns, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 2017 baseline during a tightening cycle, it confirms resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered observations, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% establishes a higher risk-free rate floor that compresses equity multiples and increases borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total employment signals structural labor market weakness, reducing consumer confidence and corporate profit forecasts, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive median returns, or the joint predictor fails to beat a single-series benchmark in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue bases, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.005.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95 indicates severe supply-side contraction that bottlenecks manufacturing output and raises input costs, forcing equity markets to price in lower earnings and producing negative returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO drops below 95, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for monetary policy shifts.
